profile.name: Victor Ciorici
profile.summary: Senior Unity Developer with 12+ years of expertise in the full development lifecycle—from concept and architecture to high-performance implementation. Specializing in gameplay systems, multiplayer architecture, and cross-platform optimization for PC, Mobile, and VR/AR.

skills.name: Unity 3D / 2D
skills.desc: Expert knowledge of Core, HDRP, and URP pipelines.

skills.name: C# / .NET
skills.desc: Advanced software architecture and design patterns.

skills.name: DOTS / ECS
skills.desc: Building scalable data-oriented systems for massive simulations.

skills.name: Shader Development
skills.desc: Custom HLSL shaders and PBR materials.

skills.name: Procedural Generation
skills.desc: Real-time generation of meshes, textures, and levels.

skills.name: Rendering Optimization
skills.desc: GPU-accelerated simulations and draw call reduction.

skills.name: Tools Development
skills.desc: Custom level editors and workflow automation utilities.

skills.name: VR / AR Foundation
skills.desc: Immersive apps for Oculus, GearVR, and AR platforms.

skills.name: PC (Steam)
skills.desc: Full lifecycle development for desktop releases.

careerTimeline.description: Leading development of high-performance mobile systems using Unity ECS and DOTS.
achievements: Optimized memory usage by 40% for low-end mobile devices.
Architected scalable multiplayer backend using Photon Fusion.

careerTimeline.description: Developed custom rendering pipelines and tools for mid-core mobile games.
Core gameplay engineering for high-traffic mobile titles.
Technical leadership and architectural oversight for the main Unity team.
Led a team of 10+ developers in creating immersive simulation environments.
Specialized in XR development and cross-platform optimization.
Full-stack game development from concept to launch on PC and Mobile.
Assisting in the development of core systems and UI frameworks.

manifesto.headline: Engineering Manifesto
manifesto.intro: A collection of core technical principles that guide every line of code I write. These are the architectural foundation of high-performance interactive systems.

tenets.title: Performance by Default
tenets.content: Optimization isn't a post-production step; it's a foundational requirement. High frame rates and low latency are the prerequisites for immersion.

tenets.title: Architecture as Art
tenets.content: Code should be as elegant as the visuals it renders. We build modular, scalable systems that survive the entropy of long-term development.

tenets.title: The DOTS Revolution
tenets.content: Embracing Data-Oriented Design to push hardware to its limits. Moving away from object-oriented bottlenecks to unlock massive scale.

tenets.title: Iterate or Die
tenets.content: Rapid prototyping combined with rigorous testing. We build, break, and refine until the system is bulletproof.

projects.title: Quantum Strike
challenges: Implementing deterministic lockstep networking in Unity's new ECS framework.
Optimizing spatial partitioning for millions of interactive particles.
Cross-platform shader parity between DirectX and Vulkan.

projects.title: Void Runner VR
challenges: Maintaining 90FPS on mobile XR hardware with high-fidelity PBR rendering.
Custom physics solver for low-latency VR interactions.
Dynamic occlusion culling for massive procedurally generated environments.

projects.title: Nexus Editor
challenges: Architecting a non-destructive level design workflow within the Unity Editor.
Automated asset validation pipelines for multi-platform deployment.
Integrating real-time telemetry data into the visual debugging suite.
